compiled_state.title: [GREEN] Implement classify subcommand in ticket CLI dispatcher
compiled_state.author: Worktree
compiled_state.description:
## Description

Implement the `classify` subcommand in the ticket CLI dispatcher and ticket-lib-api.sh library, turning task 64e4-033d RED test GREEN.

## File Impact
- plugins/dso/scripts/ticket (modify — add classify case + update _usage() string)
- plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib-api.sh (modify — add ticket_classify() function)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `ticket_classify()` added to plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib-api.sh as a bare delegation: `bash "$_TICKETLIB_DIR/classify-task.sh" "$@"; return $?`
- [ ] Function follows the ticket_validate/ticket_transition thin-wrapper pattern (no subshell with set -euo pipefail in the function body)
- [ ] `classify)` case added to dispatcher after `validate)` case (before `*)`), using: `_ensure_initialized && source ticket-lib-api.sh && _ticketlib_dispatch ticket_classify "$@" && exit $?`
- [ ] _usage() string in dispatcher updated to include `classify` with description "Classify task for routing (JSON output with model/subagent/class/complexity/priority)"
- [ ] `ticket classify <id>` accepts same arguments as classify-task.sh: single IDs, multiple IDs, --from-epic <id>
- [ ] classify-task.sh uses TICKET_CMD env var for ticket show calls — TICKET_CMD must remain injectable (do not hardcode)
- [ ] When poetry env is absent, classify emits diagnostic to stderr and exits non-zero (handled by classify-task.sh; verify passthrough)
- [ ] SOURCEABILITY CONTRACT preserved in ticket-lib-api.sh
- [ ] _TICKETLIB_DIR used for script path (not hardcoded)
- [ ] Task 64e4-033d RED tests turn GREEN after implementation
- [ ] classify-task.sh and classify-task.py are NOT modified (scope boundary)

## Notes
classify-task.sh already handles the poetry env fallback (uses system python3 if no poetry.lock). The TICKET_CMD env var injection used in tests passes through the `bash classify-task.sh` call automatically since classify-task.sh reads TICKET_CMD from the environment.
